methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $148M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Nearfield Instruments is a Series C semiconductor company founded by Roland van Vliet and Hamed Sadeghian, having raised $147.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductors (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, Positron AI, and DEEPX.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $148M raised proxy, yielding $251M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C)—produces a median valuation of $1.9B, with a range of $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (24.2x median across the three public semiconductors) cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $213M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects uncertainty in stage-progression assumptions and the heterogeneity of peer funding trajectories and market positions. The peer-median method suggests a higher anchoring point, while stage-progression yields a more conservative floor estimate.
